summary: Iteration 2 testing: Backend 100% pass (37/37 tests). Frontend testing blocked by ngrok tunnel connectivity issues. All new backend features (admin stats, profile update) working perfectly. Previous iteration tests still passing.

infrastructure_issues.issue: Public URL (https://flutter-ios-design.preview.emergentagent.com) not responding - 'The preview environment is not responding. It may be starting up.'
infrastructure_issues.error_logs: CommandError: ngrok tunnel took too long to connect, TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'body'), failed to start tunnel - session closed
infrastructure_issues.impact: Frontend testing cannot be completed via public URL
infrastructure_issues.recommendation: Check ngrok service status, restart Expo with stable tunnel, or test frontend locally on port 3000

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix ngrok tunnel connectivity issue to enable frontend testing
Verify frontend features manually once tunnel is stable: home page redesign, product selection, studio camera, admin dashboard, editable profile, premium tutorial locks
Test product selection shade picker functionality
Test camera permission flow in studio
Verify admin dashboard displays real stats for tom@gmail.com
Test profile editing (name, email, city, password) in UI
critical_code_review_comments: ✅ Backend: All endpoints working correctly (37/37 tests passed)
✅ Admin credentials updated to tom@gmail.com / Tomcle62 (role: admin)
✅ GET /api/admin/stats returns real data: total_users, total_admins, total_looks, total_messages, total_tutorials, subscriptions (free/premium), recent_users
✅ PUT /api/auth/profile updates name, email, city, password with proper validation
✅ GET /api/tutorials returns 6 tutorials with premium field: 2 premium (Contouring, Soirée Glamour), 4 free
✅ Admin stats endpoint properly restricted to admin role (403 for regular users)
✅ Profile update validates duplicate emails, password length (min 6 chars)
✅ Password update verified: old password rejected, new password works
✅ All previous tests still passing (auth, weather, tutorials, looks, chat)
⚠️ Frontend code review: All React Native components, testID attributes, and styling appear correct based on file review
⚠️ Frontend runtime testing: BLOCKED by ngrok tunnel connectivity issues
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created. Backend automatically seeds 6 tutorials (2 premium, 4 free) and admin user (tom@gmail.com) on startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully tested and working (100% pass). Frontend testing blocked by ngrok tunnel connectivity issues. All backend endpoints verified: admin login (tom@gmail.com/Tomcle62), admin stats, profile update, tutorials with premium field. Frontend code appears correct based on file review but needs runtime testing once tunnel is stable. Expo service repeatedly failing with 'ngrok tunnel took too long to connect' errors.

test_breakdown.tutorials: 3 tests (get list, get by id, invalid id)
test_breakdown.chat: 4 tests (requires auth, send message, session, history)

admin: GET /api/admin/stats - ✅ Returns real data (total_users, subscriptions, etc.)
tutorials: GET /api/tutorials - ✅ Returns 6 tutorials with premium field (2 premium, 4 free)

frontend_features_to_test: Home page: compact weather card, 'Accéder au Studio' button, Soins card with PREMIUM lock
Product selection: categories (Lèvres, Yeux, Teint, Joues) with shade picker
Studio: camera permission page on web
Tutorials: premium badge/lock on 'Contouring' and 'Soirée Glamour', free tutorials expand with 'Lancer dans le Studio' button
Profile: 'Modifier' button, editable fields (name, email, city, password), admin button for admin users
Admin dashboard: stats cards (users, looks, messages, tutorials), subscriptions chart, recent users list

ngrok_tunnel_issue_details.recommendation: This is an infrastructure/networking issue with ngrok tunnel service, not a code issue. Frontend code appears correct based on file review. Recommend: 1) Check ngrok service status, 2) Use alternative tunnel service, 3) Test frontend locally, or 4) Wait for ngrok service to stabilize

production_readiness: Backend: READY (100% tests passing). Frontend: UNKNOWN (code appears correct but runtime testing blocked by infrastructure issue)
